response.justification: The candidate is a strong, versatile senior engineer, but his profile does not match this role's core requirements. His professional C++ experience is limited to roughly seven months at National Instruments in 2014-2015, far short of the mandatory 5-10 years of hands-on C++ on Windows. His database background is MongoDB/MySQL rather than SQL Server or Oracle, and he has no Fixed Income or capital-markets systems experience — RIDE Capital was fintech-adjacent but built on Node/TypeScript. His depth is in web, Node.js, TypeScript, and DLang, which are largely orthogonal to this position.
strengths: 20 years of broad software engineering experience with strong craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, pairing)
Systems-level and low-latency experience (DLang async I/O at Sociomantic, some C/C++ at National Instruments)
Excellent communication skills evidenced by workshops, conference talks, and training courses
concerns: Only ~7 months of professional C++ experience, and not on the Windows platform — misses the primary mandatory skill
No SQL Server or Oracle experience; database background is MongoDB/MySQL
No Fixed Income, derivatives, or capital-markets domain experience; closest exposure (RIDE Capital) was a TypeScript role

key_factors.factor: Insufficient C++ experience vs. mandatory 5-10 years
No Fixed Income / capital-markets domain expertise
Strong general engineering fundamentals and communication
